title: Header Fields, Media Types, Negotiation, JSON, and Runtime Validation · Go Client Systems · Runtime Contract: Json Boundary Validation
summary: An incident collector consumes JSON and compressed text from versioned services with inconsistent media types and one changed schema. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go json boundary validation increment in a Go 1.26 net/http representation negotiation and validation boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Serialize intentional JSON and validate unknown decoded values before admitting them to trusted domain types. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Distinguish representation content coding, message delimitation, content length, and decoded size. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Predict Json Boundary Validation · Theory Json Boundary Validation
steps.instruction:
theory case — Go fleet evidence model asks for application/json and receives Content-Type application/json; charset=utf-8, Content-Encoding gzip, Vary Accept-Language, and field revision:2. Distinguish metadata layers, bound decoded bytes, and reject a changed value type. Competency probe: Serialize intentional JSON and validate unknown decoded values before admitting them to trusted domain types. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
